title: Reserved Subdomains and Namespace Governance
description:
Certain subdomains must be reserved for infrastructure use and cannot be claimed as tenant slugs to prevent collisions and security issues.

## Requirements

- Reserved list includes `auth`, `api`, `www`, and pooled hosts (e.g., `app`)
- Tenant creation rejects reserved slugs
- Edge routing treats reserved hosts as infrastructure, never as tenants
acceptance_criteria: Reserved subdomain list includes auth, api, www, and pooled host names
Tenant creation validates slugs against the reserved list and rejects conflicts
Edge routing correctly identifies reserved subdomains as infrastructure
Reserved subdomains are never resolved as tenant slugs
middleware_changes: Slug validation during tenant creation checks against reserved subdomain list. Edge routing logic treats reserved hosts as infrastructure endpoints.
implementation_notes: The reserved list should be maintained as a configuration constant, not hardcoded in multiple places. New reserved names should be easy to add as the platform evolves.
